headline: Strong evening conditions on 31m and 25m across the Americas and Europe.
highlights: 9730 kHz - Radio Romania International English to W. Europe, 1800-1900 UTC
5000 kHz - WWV time signal, continuous, excellent for propagation checks
9395 kHz - WRMI Okeechobee, varied programming to eastern North America
6070 kHz - Channel 292 Germany, hobby broadcasts to Europe
4625 kHz - UVB-76 The Buzzer, continuous, always worth a listen
changes: WRMI has adjusted some weekend programming blocks on 9955 kHz
Voice of Greece remains active on 9420 kHz after recent funding renewal
propagation_note: Low K-index tonight means quiet geomagnetic conditions and good long-path DX on 31m.
